hawkish_phrases: CPI inflation is expected to rise quite sharply towards the 2% target in the spring
Spare capacity in the economy is eliminated as activity picks up during 2021
CPI inflation is projected to be close to 2% over the second and third years
Average Weekly Earnings growth has been notably stronger than expected
dovish_phrases: If the outlook for inflation weakens, the Committee stands ready to take whatever additional action is necessary
The Committee does not intend to tighten monetary policy at least until there is clear evidence that significant progress is being made in eliminating spare capacity
A further increase in unemployment is projected over the next few quarters
GDP is expected to fall by around 4% in 2021 Q1
UK GDP...to a level around 8% lower than in 2019 Q4
summary: The MPC voted unanimously to maintain Bank Rate at 0.1% and asset purchases at £895 billion, with clear forward guidance that it will not tighten policy until spare capacity is eliminated and the 2% inflation target is sustainably achieved, while standing ready to ease further if needed.
